heading: Information with respect to certain foreign trusts
label: 6048 Information with respect to certain foreign trusts
notes: Amendments2010—Subsec. children.heading: General rule
children.label: 1 General rule
children.text: On or before the 90th day (or such later day as the Secretary may prescribe) after any reportable event, the responsible party shall provide written notice of such event to the Secretary in accordance with paragraph (2).

children.label: A
children.text: the amount of money or other property (if any) transferred to the trust in connection with the reportable event, and

children.label: B
children.text: the identity of the trust and of each trustee and beneficiary (or class of beneficiaries) of the trust.

children.label: i
children.text: the creation of any foreign trust by a United States person,

children.label: ii
children.text: the transfer of any money or property (directly or indirectly) to a foreign trust by a United States person, including a transfer by reason of death, and

children.label: I
children.text: the decedent was treated as the owner of any portion of a foreign trust under the rules of subpart E of part I of subchapter J of chapter 1, or

children.label: II
children.text: any portion of a foreign trust was included in the gross estate of the decedent.

children.heading: Fair market value sales
children.label: i Fair market value sales
children.text: Subparagraph (A)(ii) shall not apply to any transfer of property to a trust in exchange for consideration of at least the fair market value of the transferred property. For purposes of the preceding sentence, consideration other than cash shall be taken into account at its fair market value and the rules of section 679(a)(3) shall apply.

children.heading: Deferred compensation and charitable trusts
children.label: ii Deferred compensation and charitable trusts
I
children.text: described in section 402(b), 404(a)(4), or 404A, or

children.label: II
children.text: determined by the Secretary to be described in section 501(c)(3).

children.label: A
children.text: the grantor in the case of the creation of an inter vivos trust,

children.label: B
children.text: the transferor in the case of a reportable event described in paragraph (3)(A)(ii) other than a transfer by reason of death, and

children.label: C
children.text: the executor of the decedent’s estate in any other case.

children.heading: United States owner of foreign trust
children.label: b United States owner of foreign trust
A
children.text: such trust makes a return for such year which sets forth a full and complete accounting of all trust activities and operations for the year, the name of the United States agent for such trust, and such other information as the Secretary may prescribe, and

children.label: B
children.text: such trust furnishes such information as the Secretary may prescribe to each United States person (i) who is treated as the owner of any portion of such trust or (ii) who receives (directly or indirectly) any distribution from the trust.

children.heading: Trusts not having United States agent
children.label: 2 Trusts not having United States agent

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: If the rules of this paragraph apply to any foreign trust, the determination of amounts required to be taken into account with respect to such trust by a United States person under the rules of subpart E of part I of subchapter J of chapter 1 shall be determined by the Secretary.

children.label: i
children.text: any request by the Secretary to examine records or produce testimony related to the proper treatment of amounts required to be taken into account under the rules referred to in subparagraph (A), or

children.label: ii
children.text: any summons by the Secretary for such records or testimony.

children.heading: Other rules to apply
children.label: C Other rules to apply
children.text: Rules similar to the rules of paragraphs (2) and (4) of section 6038A(e) shall apply for purposes of this paragraph.

children.heading: Reporting by United States beneficiaries of foreign trusts
children.label: c Reporting by United States beneficiaries of foreign trusts
A
children.text: the name of such trust,

children.label: B
children.text: the aggregate amount of the distributions so received from such trust during such taxable year, and

children.label: C
children.text: such other information as the Secretary may prescribe.

children.heading: Inclusion in income if records not provided
children.label: 2 Inclusion in income if records not provided

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: If adequate records are not provided to the Secretary to determine the proper treatment of any distribution from a foreign trust, such distribution shall be treated as an accumulation distribution includible in the gross income of the distributee under chapter 1. To the extent provided in regulations, the preceding sentence shall not apply if the foreign trust elects to be subject to rules similar to the rules of subsection (b)(2)(B).

children.heading: Application of accumulation distribution rules
children.label: B Application of accumulation distribution rules
children.text: For purposes of applying section 668 in a case to which subparagraph (A) applies, the applicable number of years for purposes of section 668(a) shall be ½ of the number of years the trust has been in existence.

children.heading: Determination of whether United States person makes transfer or receives distribution
children.label: 1 Determination of whether United States person makes transfer or receives distribution
children.text: For purposes of this section, in determining whether a United States person makes a transfer to, or receives a distribution from, a foreign trust, the fact that a portion of such trust is treated as owned by another person under the rules of subpart E of part I of subchapter J of chapter 1 shall be disregarded.

children.heading: Domestic trusts with foreign activities
children.label: 2 Domestic trusts with foreign activities
children.text: To the extent provided in regulations, a trust which is a United States person shall be treated as a foreign trust for purposes of this section and section 6677 if such trust has substantial activities, or holds substantial property, outside the United States.

children.heading: Time and manner of filing information
children.label: 3 Time and manner of filing information
children.text: Any notice or return required under this section shall be made at such time and in such manner as the Secretary shall prescribe.

children.heading: Modification of return requirements
children.label: 4 Modification of return requirements
children.text: The Secretary is authorized to suspend or modify any requirement of this section if the Secretary determines that the United States has no significant tax interest in obtaining the required information.

children.heading: United States person’s return must be consistent with trust return or Secretary notified of inconsistency
children.label: 5 United States person’s return must be consistent with trust return or Secretary notified of inconsistency
children.text: Rules similar to the rules of section 6034A(c) shall apply to items reported by a trust under subsection (b)(1)(B) and to United States persons referred to in such subsection.
